Summary: Capture OS information from Server Under Test
Description: Goal: Capture core Windows OS profile for the SUT and store in structured output. Scope: OS name and edition, Windows version/build, architecture, hostname, install date. Acceptance: 1) OS name/edition/version/build captured. 2) Architecture captured. 3) Hostname captured. 4) Install date captured. 5) Output is structured (JSON). 6) Missing fields recorded as Unavailable instead of failing.

Summary: Capture NVIDIA driver and GPU details from Server Under Test
Description: Goal: Record installed NVIDIA display driver and associated GPU details on the SUT. Scope: driver version/date/provider, GPU name/model, installed GPU count. Acceptance: 1) NVIDIA driver version captured. 2) Driver date/provider captured. 3) GPU model/device name captured. 4) If NVIDIA not present, fields recorded as Not installed. 5) Output format consistent with other captured fields.

Summary: Capture hard drive inventory and infer RAID configuration
Description: Goal: Enumerate physical and logical storage and infer likely RAID usage from topology differences. Scope: physical drive count/model/size/type, logical drives and sizes, inferred RAID flag based on physical vs logical discrepancy. Acceptance: 1) Physical drives enumerated with model/size/type. 2) Logical volumes enumerated with drive letter/size. 3) RAID inference flag set when counts differ. 4) Human-readable status label provided (RAID likely configured or No RAID detected). 5) Output explicitly marks RAID result as inferred. 6) Structured output generated.

Summary: Capture registry diff against default installation baseline
Description: Goal: Identify 7thSense registry deviations on the SUT relative to a known clean baseline. Scope: baseline snapshot from clean machine, current SUT snapshot, diff of added/removed/modified keys/values under 7thSense subtree only. Acceptance: 1) Baseline snapshot process exists. 2) Current SUT snapshot captured. 3) Added/removed/modified diff generated. 4) Non-7thSense registry paths excluded. 5) Diff output structured by change type. 6) Baseline refresh process documented. 7) If no differences, explicit No non-default registry changes detected result.

Summary: Capture non-standard PCI hardware (audio and capture cards)
Description: Goal: Detect optional/non-standard PCI add-in hardware that may affect performance outcomes. Scope: enumerate PCI devices, highlight multimedia audio and capture classes, include vendor and PCI IDs, de-emphasize standard platform devices. Acceptance: 1) PCI devices enumerated. 2) Audio and capture devices explicitly highlighted. 3) Other non-standard add-in devices included. 4) Name/vendor/device class/PCI IDs captured per device. 5) Standard bridges/controllers filtered or clearly de-emphasized. 6) Structured output generated.

Summary: Add System Configuration panel to Performance Dashboard
Description: Goal: Show captured SUT configuration alongside performance data in a dedicated dashboard panel. Scope: OS, NVIDIA, storage/RAID, registry diff summary, PCI inventory, and empty-state handling. Acceptance: 1) New System Configuration panel is available in main dashboard navigation. 2) Panel displays all captured sections with clear labels. 3) Visual separation from performance panels. 4) Not yet captured state displayed when data absent. 5) Registry section supports summary with expandable detail. 6) Layout reviewed for consistency with existing dashboard style.

Summary: Implement remote collection mechanism for SUT configuration data
Description: Goal: Enable monitor app on a separate machine to retrieve SUT configuration data without manual interaction on the SUT during routine use. Scope: define capture agent/service model, output location or interface, remote retrieval flow, failure handling, security considerations, and clean removability after testing. Acceptance: 1) Remote access mechanism defined and implemented. 2) Output stored in known configurable network-accessible location or endpoint. 3) Monitor app retrieves latest data automatically once tool is present on SUT. 4) Access method documented (share/endpoint/polling). 5) Capture tooling and artifacts removable, restoring pre-test state. 6) Unreachable/unavailable states handled with status messaging, not crash. 7) Security and access controls documented. 8) Tested with monitor on physically separate machine.
